Male Protagonist – The Aspiring Singer-Songwriter
Look & Build (base design)

Age: Early 20s

Build: Lean, wiry — the type who looks like he skips meals but carries quiet energy.

Hair: Tousled dark brown, mid-length, slightly messy as if he’s always running from one gig to another.

Eyes: Warm hazel, expressive and full of quiet optimism.

Skin: Light olive tone with faint signs of being outdoors a lot (sun exposure).

Posture: Relaxed, slightly slouched, but straightens up when he’s holding his guitar.

Signature Item: His guitar case, scuffed but well-loved, covered in stickers and scribbles.

Costume Set 1 – Before Transformation (Everyday Look)

Top: A faded charcoal-gray hoodie layered over a threadbare band T-shirt. Hoodie sleeves often pushed up.

Bottoms: Slim-fit black jeans, worn at the knees.

Shoes: Old canvas sneakers (Converse-like), one lace mismatched.

Accessories: a thin leather bracelet on his right wrist.

Overall Style: Struggling musician — practical, low-maintenance, but with little touches that show his love for music (band tee, bracelet).

Costume Set 2 – Festival Stage (Transformation Look)

Top: A fitted black leather jacket with silver zipper details, open to reveal a deep burgundy V-neck shirt beneath.

Bottoms: Dark slim-fit jeans, but clean and stylish now.

Shoes: Black boots with metallic accents that catch the stage lights.

Accessories:

Studded guitar strap.

A necklace with a guitar pick pendant (gifted by the female protagonist — optional narrative detail).

Overall Style: Rockstar confidence — same silhouette as before but upgraded, sharper, and commanding presence under the spotlights.

Defining Expression: The resilient half-smile.

His eyes are warm, slightly tired but always glimmering with quiet determination.

His mouth curves into a small, lopsided smile — not cocky, not polished, but the smile of someone who takes life’s blows and shrugs them off with humor.

He often tilts his head slightly when he smiles, like he’s letting you in on a private joke or a stubborn hope he refuses to let go of.

Expression reads: “Yeah, life’s tough. But I’m tougher.”

✨ Design Evolution Notes:

Both characters should look like “before” and “after” versions of themselves, not different people.

His look evolves from scrappy/laid-back → sharp rocker.

Her look evolves from muted/blending in → radiant/star presence.

Their final silhouettes complement each other: he’s dark, sharp, edgy; she’s bright, flowing, radiant. Together, they balance on stage visually as well as musically.
